issue_summary: Automatically rewrite disjunctions when internal gaps matter
javadoc_before: /** * Constructor functions for {@link IntervalsSource} types * * These sources implement minimum-interval algorithms taken from the paper * * Efficient Optimally Lazy Algorithms for Minimal-Interval Semantics */
javadoc_after: /** * Constructor functions for {@link IntervalsSource} types * * These sources implement minimum-interval algorithms taken from the paper * * Efficient Optimally Lazy Algorithms for Minimal-Interval Semantics * * By default, sources that are sensitive to internal gaps (e.g. PHRASE and MAXGAPS) will * rewrite their sub-sources so that disjunctions of different lengths are pulled up * to the top of the interval tree. For example, PHRASE(or(PHRASE("a", "b", "c"), "b"), "c") * will automatically rewrite itself to OR(PHRASE("a", "b", "c", "c"), PHRASE("b", "c")) * to ensure that documents containing "b c" are matched. This can lead to less efficient * queries, as more terms need to be loaded (for example, the "c" iterator above is loaded * twice), so if you care more about speed than about accuracy you can use the * {@link #or(boolean, IntervalsSource...)} factory method to prevent rewriting. */
